stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the core domain of large language models (LLMs) and transformer architectures.
The associated tags indicate relevance to text generation and the use of safetensors.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the core NLP/LLM domain, specifically using the 'text-generation' pipeline.
The model is associated with key technologies like transformers and safetensors.

stories.title: A revolutionary cancer treatment could transform autoimmune disease
stories.author: Amber Dance, Knowable Magazine
bullet_points: CAR T cell therapy is being investigated for treating autoimmune diseases.
The therapy aims to 'reset' the immune system, a significant biological intervention.
The research represents a potential paradigm shift in treating complex immune disorders.

stories.title: Researcher says Microsoft secretly built a backdoor into BitLocker
stories.author: techspot.com via oceanhaiyang
bullet_points: Allegation claims Microsoft secretly implemented a backdoor within the BitLocker encryption feature.
The story is based on claims made by a researcher, suggesting a significant security vulnerability.
This topic is highly relevant as it concerns core operating system security and data privacy.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights a trending model, DeepSeek-R1, on the Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and utilizes transformers and safetensors.
It is associated with the deepseek_v3 family, indicating its relevance in the conversational AI space.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating a focus on large language models.
The associated tags (transformers, pytorch, safetensors) confirm its relevance to modern deep learning infrastructure.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, has integrated MLX for enhanced performance.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon devices, optimizing AI workloads on macOS.
The feature is currently available in preview, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models come in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
The partnership involves Ollama, OpenAI, and ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools) to enhance open-source safety.

stories.title: The US is betting on AI to catch insider trading in prediction markets
stories.author: Kate Knibbs, wired.com
bullet_points: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) is actively leveraging AI.
The AI is being deployed to monitor and detect insider trading activities.
The focus area for this surveillance is prediction markets.

stories.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement is getting messy as judge delays approval
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: Anthropic is involved in a significant copyright settlement valued at $1.5B.
The approval of this historic settlement is facing delays from the judge.
Legal disputes have arisen, with accusations that lawyers rushed the settlement to secure substantial fees.

stories.title: Designing Datacenter Power Delivery Hierarchies for the AI Era
stories.author: Ricardo Bianchini
bullet_points: AI accelerator demand is driving extreme increases in datacenter rack power density, projected near 1MW by 2027.
This high density presents a critical design challenge, risking stranded power if the power delivery hierarchy is not future-proofed.
Future datacenter designs must ensure power delivery efficiency across long operational lifetimes and multiple hardware generations.

stories.title: Prospective multi-pathogen disease forecasting using autonomous LLM-guided tree search
stories.author: Zahra Shamsi
bullet_points: Introduces an autonomous system leveraging LLM-guided tree search for disease forecasting.
Aims to automate the traditionally labor-intensive process of curating and optimizing infectious disease models.
Addresses the scalability bottleneck in public health forecasting for granular geographies and emerging pathogens.

stories.title: FORGE: Self-Evolving Agent Memory With No Weight Updates via Population Broadcast
stories.author: Marzia Zaman
bullet_points: Introduces FORGE, a novel protocol for self-evolving agent memory in LLM agents.
Allows agents to improve decision-making by generating and reusing knowledge artifacts from failed trajectories without requiring weight updates (gradient descent).
Employs a population-based, staged approach wrapping a Reflexion-style inner loop using a dedicated reflection agent.

stories.title: A Unified Generative-AI Framework for Smart Energy Infrastructure: Intelligent Gas Distribution, Utility Billing, Carbon Analytics, and Quantum-Inspired Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a unified Generative AI framework for smart energy infrastructure management.
Integrates concepts like smart metering, generative AI, and quantum-inspired optimization.
Addresses critical utility functions including gas distribution, billing, and carbon analytics.

stories.title: AI-Mediated Communication Can Steer Collective Opinion
stories.author: Sandra Wachter
bullet_points: Investigates how AI-mediated communication (using LLMs on platforms like LinkedIn and X) influences collective opinion formation.
Addresses a gap in research by focusing on AI's role in human-to-human opinion exchange, rather than just human-AI interaction.
Highlights the growing integration of generative AI into online social platforms for content polishing and context provision.

stories.title: FORGE: Self-Evolving Agent Memory With No Weight Updates via Population Broadcast
stories.author: Marzia Zaman
bullet_points: Introduces FORGE, a method for LLM agents to self-evolve memory without requiring traditional weight updates (gradient descent).
Uses a population-based protocol and a Reflexion-style inner loop to convert failed agent trajectories into reusable knowledge artifacts.
Focuses on improving decision-making in hierarchical ReAct agents by injecting natural-language memory derived from failures.

stories.title: A Unified Generative-AI Framework for Smart Energy Infrastructure: Intelligent Gas Distribution, Utility Billing, Carbon Analytics, and Quantum-Inspired Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a unified Generative AI framework to modernize energy infrastructure management.
Integrates smart metering, generative AI, and quantum-inspired optimization for complex utility problems.
Addresses critical industry needs including intelligent gas distribution, utility billing, and carbon analytics.

stories.title: Universal Magnetic Structure Prediction from Atomic Coordinates with Near-Experimental Accuracy
stories.author: Mingda Li
bullet_points: Introduces Magnetic Structure Network (MSN), an E(3) equivariant graph neural network.
MSN aims to predict magnetic structures from atomic coordinates with near-experimental accuracy.
Addresses a key challenge in materials science by handling complex noncollinear and incommensurate magnetic orders.

stories.title: FORGE: Self-Evolving Agent Memory With No Weight Updates via Population Broadcast
stories.author: Marzia Zaman
bullet_points: Introduces FORGE, a new protocol for self-evolving agent memory in LLM agents.
Allows agents to improve decision-making by generating reusable knowledge artifacts from failed trajectories without requiring weight updates (gradient descent).
Employs a population-based, staged approach wrapping a Reflexion-style inner loop using the same underlying LLM.

stories.title: A Unified Generative-AI Framework for Smart Energy Infrastructure: Intelligent Gas Distribution, Utility Billing, Carbon Analytics, and Quantum-Inspired Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a unified Generative AI framework to modernize energy infrastructure management.
Integrates smart metering, generative AI, and quantum-inspired optimization for utility operations.
Addresses key industry challenges including intelligent gas distribution, billing, and carbon analytics.

stories.title: Fully Open Meditron: An Auditable Pipeline for Clinical LLMs
stories.author: Mary-Anne Hartley
bullet_points: The paper introduces 'Fully Open Meditron,' a pipeline designed to address the opacity of current LLM-based Clinical Decision Support Systems (CDSS).
A key problem identified is that existing 'open' models are often only open-weight, lacking transparency regarding data provenance and training pipelines.
The concept of 'Fully Open' (FO) models, which expose the entire training stack end-to-end, is presented as a necessary standard for rigorous validation in medical AI.

stories.title: Subagents and web search in Claude Code
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has integrated support for advanced AI features within Claude Code.
The new capabilities include the implementation of subagents for complex task decomposition.
Web search functionality has been added, enhancing Claude Code's ability to access real-time information.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code capabilities within the Ollama ecosystem.
It facilitates the use of proprietary models like Claude alongside open-source models.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex, when integrated, can read, modify, and execute code within a user's working directory.
The system supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Revamped Siri will reportedly offer autodeleting chats
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Apple's revamped Siri for iOS 27 will feature a chatbot-like interface.
A key privacy feature of the new Siri will be the option for autodeleting chat histories.
The update is positioned as Apple leveraging its privacy reputation against AI competitors.

stories.title: ElliQ is a surprisingly helpful companion robot for older adults
stories.author: Sheena Vasani
bullet_points: ElliQ is a companion robot designed to assist older adults, particularly those with conditions like Parkinson's.
The robot aims to encourage crucial activities such as exercise, socialization, and hobbies to help manage chronic diseases.
The article highlights the need for lifestyle rebalancing for older adults as medication effectiveness declines.

stories.title: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok settle suit over harm to students
stories.author: Terrence O’Brien
bullet_points: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok have reached a settlement in a landmark lawsuit.
The suit alleged that social media addiction caused significant financial harm to public schools.
The core claims centered on social media disrupting learning and contributing to a student mental health crisis.

stories.title: Three's a party: US, China, and now Russia are on the prowl in GEO
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: The article discusses the increasing surveillance activity in Geosynchronous Orbit (GEO) by the US, China, and Russia.
It highlights a technical vulnerability where satellites are not as stealthy as previously assumed against the backdrop of space.
The piece suggests a shift from covert operations to more visible, active monitoring in space.

stories.title: IVGT: Implicit Visual Geometry Transformer for Neural Scene Representation
stories.author: Jiwen Lu
bullet_points: IVGT introduces a new Implicit Visual Geometry Transformer for 3D scene representation.
It addresses the challenge of reconstructing coherent 3D geometry from unposed, multi-view images.
The method moves beyond explicit pointmap prediction, aiming for continuous and coherent geometric modeling.

stories.title: A Generative AI Framework for Intelligent Utility Billing CO 2 Analytics and Sustainable Resource Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas Pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a generative AI framework to address the need for transparent, carbon-aware utility billing.
The system integrates a generative AI agent to draft natural-language billing statements from structured data.
It includes a transformer-based forecaster to manage load scheduling against grid stress and emissions constraints.

stories.title: AI-Mediated Communication Can Steer Collective Opinion
stories.author: Sandra Wachter
bullet_points: The research investigates how AI-mediated communication influences collective opinion formation among humans.
It addresses a gap in prior research, which focused more on individual opinion shaping during human-AI interactions.
The study is relevant given the increasing integration of LLMs into major social platforms like LinkedIn and X.

stories.title: Offline Semantic Guidance for Efficient Vision-Language-Action Policy Distillation
stories.author: Yishun Lu
bullet_points: Addresses the high computational cost of large Vision-Language-Action (VLA) policies used in robotics.
Introduces VLA-AD, a distillation framework that uses a Vision-Language Model as an offline semantic supervisor.
Aims to transfer knowledge from large, high-performing teacher policies to lightweight student policies for real-time control.

stories.title: Layer Equivalence Is Not a Property of Layers Alone: How You Test Redundancy Changes What You Find
stories.author: Gabriel Garcia
bullet_points: The concept of 'layer equivalence' in transformer models is nuanced and depends heavily on the specific testing methodology used.
Two common tests, 'replacement' and 'interchange,' are distinct measures of layer redundancy, even though both are output-grounded probes.
The choice of testing protocol (e.g., replacement vs. interchange) can lead to significant discrepancies in which layers are deemed safe for pruning in pretrained transformers.

stories.title: Evaluating Design Video Generation: Metrics for Compositional Fidelity
stories.author: Purvanshi Mehta
bullet_points: Addresses the lack of standardized evaluation metrics for generative video models used in design animation.
Highlights the unique constraints of design animation, such as prescribed motion, stable regions, and layout preservation.
Proposes a fully automated evaluation framework structured across four key dimensions.

stories.title: A Generative AI Framework for Intelligent Utility Billing CO 2 Analytics and Sustainable Resource Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas Pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a generative AI framework to automate the creation of natural-language utility billing statements.
Addresses the industry need for utilities to provide customers with carbon-attributed data for every unit of energy (kWh).
Integrates a transformer-based forecaster to manage load scheduling against grid stress and emission constraints.

stories.title: Dynamics-Level Watermarking of Flow Matching Models with Random Codes
stories.author: Shuchan Wang
bullet_points: Introduces a novel dynamics-level watermarking technique for generative models, specifically flow matching models.
The watermark is embedded directly into the continuous dynamics (velocity field) rather than model weights or outputs.
The method utilizes random coding over a continuous channel, allowing for black-box message recovery during detection.

stories.title: Layer Equivalence Is Not a Property of Layers Alone: How You Test Redundancy Changes What You Find
stories.author: Gabriel Garcia
bullet_points: The study highlights a critical distinction between 'replacement' and 'interchange' tests when evaluating transformer layer equivalence for model compression.
These two tests, while both being output-grounded probes, do not necessarily yield the same results regarding layer redundancy.
The protocol gap between these tests can lead to significant discrepancies in which layers are deemed safe to prune in pretrained transformers.

stories.title: Artificial Aphasias in Lesioned Language Models
stories.author: Cory Shain
bullet_points: The research proposes an 'aphasia-inspired' technique to study the functional organization of Language Models (LMs).
This method involves 'lesioning' (zeroing out) specific model parameters to observe functional impact.
The intervention effects are measured against clinical aphasia symptom profiles, linking neuroscience to AI.

stories.title: A Generative AI Framework for Intelligent Utility Billing CO 2 Analytics and Sustainable Resource Optimisation
stories.author: Thomas Pruefer
bullet_points: Proposes a generative AI framework to automate the creation of natural-language utility billing statements.
The system integrates carbon footprint tracking (CO2 per kWh) and load scheduling against grid constraints.
The framework combines a generative AI agent with a transformer-based forecaster for end-to-end utility optimization.

stories.title: AI-Mediated Communication Can Steer Collective Opinion
stories.author: Sandra Wachter
bullet_points: The research investigates how AI, specifically LLMs, influences collective opinion formation in human-to-human online communication.
AI is increasingly integrated into social platforms (e.g., LinkedIn, X) to polish posts and provide content context.
The study addresses a gap in prior research by focusing on AI's mediating role in group opinion shifts, rather than just individual interactions.

stories.title: Layer Equivalence Is Not a Property of Layers Alone: How You Test Redundancy Changes What You Find
stories.author: Gabriel Garcia
bullet_points: The study highlights a critical distinction between 'replacement' and 'interchange' when testing transformer layer equivalence for model compression.
These two tests, though related, do not always yield the same results regarding which layers are safe to prune.
The 'protocol gap' between these evaluation methods can lead to significant differences in pruning decisions on pretrained transformers.

stories.title: Artificial Aphasias in Lesioned Language Models
stories.author: Cory Shain
bullet_points: The research proposes an 'aphasia-inspired' technique to study the functional organization of Language Models (LMs).
The method involves 'lesioning' (zeroing out) specific model parameters and observing the resulting effects.
The intervention's impact is measured against clinical aphasia symptom profiles, linking brain damage concepts to AI models.

stories.title: Argus: Evidence Assembly for Scalable Deep Research Agents
stories.author: Xinyu Wang
bullet_points: The paper addresses a limitation in current deep research agents: parallel search methods often duplicate evidence instead of finding complementary pieces.
The proposed method, 'Argus,' focuses on Evidence Assembly to improve the quality and completeness of deep research answers.
This work targets scaling inference time compute in research agents while managing the aggregation context size for better performance.

stories.title: Confirming Correct, Missing the Rest: LLM Tutoring Agents Struggle Where Feedback Matters Most
stories.author: Tiffany Barnes
bullet_points: The study evaluates LLM tutoring agents on their ability to provide nuanced feedback, specifically distinguishing between correct, valid but suboptimal, and incorrect student solutions.
It addresses a critical gap in current LLM applications: the lack of tested diagnostic precision when LLMs are integrated into Intelligent Tutoring Systems (ITS).
The research uses a large benchmark (10,836 pairs) in propositional logic, grounded by knowledge-graph truth, to test seven different LLM feedback agents.

stories.title: Show HN: Semble – Code search for agents that uses 98% fewer tokens than grep
stories.author: Bibabomas
bullet_points: Semble is an open-source tool designed to improve code search for AI agents.
It addresses the high token usage and inefficiency of agents falling back to traditional tools like grep on large codebases.
The tool claims to use 98% fewer tokens than grep while maintaining retrieval quality.

stories.title: I Ran AI Models Directly in the Browser and Measured What It Did to Core Web Vitals
stories.author: mspk97
bullet_points: Explores running AI models directly within the web browser.
Focuses on measuring the impact of in-browser AI on Core Web Vitals.
Addresses the growing trend of shipping AI features into web applications.

stories.title: OpenSRE: Build Your Own AI Incident-Investigation Agent
stories.author: arshtechpro
bullet_points: Introduces 'OpenSRE,' a tool designed for AI incident investigation.
Addresses the limitation of current AI coding tools, which primarily focus on code generation.
Targets the complex and stressful domain of post-incident analysis in SRE/DevOps.

stories.title: Introducing MAIS: A Privacy-Preserving Identity Standard for AI Agents on Midnight
stories.author: mzf11125
bullet_points: Introduction of MAIS, a new privacy-preserving identity standard.
The standard is designed specifically for AI agents operating on the Midnight Network.
Addresses the current lack of a standardized method for AI agents to verify their identity.

stories.title:
“What good is AI if it stops working the moment the internet dies?
Built an offline Gemma 4 farm doctor for real-world rural use.”
stories.author: sreejit_
bullet_points: Addresses the critical limitation of cloud-dependent AI models (internet dependency).
Presents a practical, real-world application: an offline 'Farm Doctor' powered by Gemma 4.
Highlights the trend toward deploying smaller, robust, edge-AI solutions for rural/low-connectivity environments.
